LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT (2ND CAUSE OF ACTION) IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO PLEAD THE STATUTORY BASIS FOR DIRECT LIABILITY OR FACTS ESTABLISHING A BASIS FOR VICARIOUS LIABILITY. MUNOZ V. CITY OF UNION (2004) 120 CAL. APP.4TH 1077, 1111-1115 CONTEMPLATES THAT THE NEGLIGENT EMPLOYEE BE IDENTIFIED, IF NOT NAMED, IN THE ACTION WHERE PLAINTIFF RELIES ON RESPONDENT SUPERIOR. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-26-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Mark Lipton of the Office of the City Attorney (554.4218) for defendant and moving party

No other appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT (2ND CAUSE OF ACTION). The demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend for plaintiff to plead the statutory basis for direct liability or facts establishing a basis for vicarious liability. Munoz v. City of Union (2004) 120 Cal. App.4th 1077, 1111-1115 contemplates that the negligent employee be identified, if not named, in the action where plaintiff relies on respondent superior. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
